Description:
A JAMA Surgery study projects autonomous vehicles could prevent over 1 million injuries by 2035.

Motor vehicle crashes currently generate 2.6 million ED visits per year and $470 billion in economic costs. That's a massive revenue stream for trauma centers, orthopedic surgeons, rehabilitation facilities, and emergency departments.

This is demand elimination through technology — not through drugs or behavior change, but through removing the human error that causes 93% of crashes.

Health systems rarely model autonomous vehicles into their strategic plans. They should. Trauma is one of the highest-margin service lines. When crash injuries decline 3.6% — and that's the conservative estimate — the downstream impact on ED volumes, surgical cases, and rehab admissions is significant.

GLP-1s reduce metabolic disease demand. AI reduces knowledge and triage demand. And autonomous vehicles could reduce trauma demand. The interesting thing is that two of those three forces come from completely outside healthcare.

https://buff.ly/vQz8rcn

#DemandElimination #DrYou #HealthcareDisruption #FutureOfHealth
Author Headline: Physician • TED/Keynote Speaker | Healthcare is getting better, and getting smaller — I help leaders prepare

Description:
🚗🤖 Are autonomous vehicles truly ready for adoption in Amman City, and are we prepared to let go of the steering wheel and trust AI amidst our city’s heavy congestion? 🤔🏙️🚦✨
In Amman 🇯🇴, where 55.04% of commuters spend 60+ minutes daily on the road ⏳, the transition toward Autonomous Vehicles (AVs) and Shared Autonomous Vehicles (SAVs) is not merely technological — it is a socio-technical transformation grounded in public trust, regulatory clarity, and infrastructure readiness 👥🏗️✨. Regional momentum is accelerating 🚀: in Dubai 🇦🇪, Expo 2020 autonomous shuttles completed 180,000+ trips with zero accidents 🛡️🚍; in NEOM 🇸🇦, plans target 100% autonomous transit, aiming to reduce emissions by 20% 🌱 and travel times by 30% ⏱️. Comparable structured trials are advancing in Singapore 🇸🇬 and Malaysia 🇲🇾, particularly within mixed-traffic environments 🚦.
I am pleased to share our latest publication in the International Journal of Transportation Science and Technology (IJTST) (Q1) Published by Elsevier 📘✨, a collaborative effort with my esteemed colleagues and friends, Dr. Haitham A. Al Hasanat, and Dr. Dania Alothman 🤝🎓✨.
📄 Adoption of autonomous and shared autonomous vehicles: a multi-criteria decision-making approach 
🔗 https://lnkd.in/eaABZd8f
⭐ This is the first study ever to develop and empirically implement a fully integrated hierarchical model synthesizing UTAUT and TAM Models within a Fuzzy AHP-based MCDM framework for Jordan. The study moves beyond descriptive correlations toward quantified, expert-weighted determinants of adoption 📊⚙️🔍.
Empirical findings reveal that User Perception (UP) — particularly Performance Expectancy (PE) — is the dominant adoption driver 📈. While 72.09% indicate willingness to adopt, 50.72% cite safety as the primary barrier ⚠️, and 93.8% remain unaware of future implementation plans 📢. Accordingly, we advocate a Gradual Integration Approach (GIA) 🔄 — strongly favored by non-drivers (weight = 0.512) — enabling synchronized infrastructure development, institutional preparedness, and societal trust in cities such as Amman 🏙️🛡️.

Because the future of mobility is not shaped by automation alone — it is shaped by human perception and evidence-based planning 👥📊🏛️✨.

#AutonomousVehicles #SmartCities #TransportationEngineering #MCDM #FAHP #Elsevier #Q1Journal #Amman #Innovation #Jordan 🚀📈

Welcome to the first edition of 𝗗𝗠𝗦 𝗦𝘆𝗻𝘁𝗵𝗲𝘁𝗶𝗰 𝗗𝗮𝘁𝗮 𝗣𝗶𝗹𝗹𝘀!

We are thrilled to launch this space dedicated to exploring the world of synthetic data for in-cabin monitoring, perception AI, and automotive safety. 

If you are an automotive engineer, product leader, or AI developer, make sure to hit 𝙎𝙪𝙗𝙨𝙘𝙧𝙞𝙗𝙚 so you don't miss future deep dives!

For our inaugural article, we are tackling one of the biggest headaches facing OEMs and Tier-1 suppliers today: The global divergence of safety protocols.

A system tuned perfectly for one region can now trigger endless false positives, or fail outright, in another.

In this first edition, we break down:
🌍 The core technical differences between Euro NCAP, C-NCAP, and NHTSA/IIHS.
⚠️ Why Driver Monitoring (DMS) is diverging, while Occupant Monitoring (OMS) is converging.
🛠️ How top engineering teams are using synthetic data to build modular, "protocol-agnostic" architectures that comply everywhere.

We’d love to hear from you: 

How is your team handling the fragmentation of global safety standards? 

Are you building region-specific logic, or aiming for a unified architecture? 

Let us know in the comments below! 👇

An intersection in San Francisco recently turned into a quiet experiment in autonomy.

Several self-driving taxis aligned perfectly.

Too perfectly.

For a brief moment, they created a polite deadlock, each system calculating, none asserting. No aggression. No improvisation. Just algorithms waiting for clarity.

It resolved quickly.

But it exposed something deeper.

Urban mobility isn’t just rules and geometry.
It’s negotiation.
It’s subtle signals.
It’s imperfect humans making micro-judgments in ambiguous situations.

Autonomous systems excel in structured environments.

Cities are not structured. They are adaptive.

The real challenge isn’t object detection or route optimization.

It’s encoding judgment under uncertainty.

We’re not just teaching machines to drive.

We’re teaching them to participate in social systems.

And that’s a different level of complexity.

#AutonomousVehicles #AI #UrbanMobility #SystemsThinking #Innovation

Source 🙏 Granny&Smith

The latest edition of the Automotive Test and Measurement Focus Newsletter from TOYO covers the most critical trends shaping automotive validation and compliance worldwide.  In this issue:

 • Global certification and international drive cycle compliance
 • Four-wheel steering validation and wheel-level precision testing
 • The importance of lithium battery safety and lifecycle testing
 • Advanced OTA test solutions for connected vehicles

As electrification, software-defined platforms, and vehicle connectivity accelerate, robust testing strategies are more important than ever.  If you’re involved in EV development, ADAS validation, chassis systems, or automotive wireless performance, this edition is especially for you.

Do let us know if you have any comments, questions or suggestions for future newsletter topics.

IMO, a key focus for identity and data protection for organizations in 2026 should be device and application management (MDM/MAM).

Quite simply, if you don't manage the devices and/or applications that access your organizational data, then you DO NOT control your data. If you don't manage from which devices organizational users can authenticate for access, then you DO NOT control your identities.

#identityprotection #dataprotection #devicemanagement
